findings.evidence: jobs.py:10 calls `client.fetch(url, timeout=60)` with the comment 'give it a full minute'. After the PR, `timeout` is now milliseconds, so `timeout=60` passes `timeout_s=0.06` (60ms) to the transport instead of 60s — a 1000x reduction. The caller was never updated to use `timeout=60000`.
findings.why_tests_missed_it: test_fetch_report_uses_client (test_client.py:27-31) exercises this call path but only asserts `result['status'] == 200`; it never checks `ft.last['timeout_s']`, so the wrong timeout value passes silently.
findings.fix: Update jobs.py:10 to `client.fetch(url, timeout=60000)` (60 000ms = 60s), and add an assertion in test_fetch_report_uses_client that `ft.last['timeout_s'] == 60`.

findings.evidence: test_fetch_report_uses_client uses FakeTransport but only checks the HTTP status of the result, not the timeout_s value. The test therefore cannot fail regardless of what timeout is forwarded to the transport, masking the units mismatch in jobs.py.
findings.why_tests_missed_it: The assertion `assert result['status'] == 200` is vacuous with respect to the timeout contract; FakeTransport always returns status 200 regardless of timeout_s.
findings.fix: Add `assert ft.last['timeout_s'] == 60` to test_fetch_report_uses_client so any caller-side timeout miscount becomes immediately visible.

findings.evidence: The public signature of `Client.fetch` changed from `fetch(self, url, timeout=30)` to `fetch(self, url, timeout=30000)`. Any external caller (outside this snapshot) that passes a numeric seconds value — e.g. `fetch(url, timeout=5)` for a 5-second timeout — silently gets 5ms after this change. The surface map shows `jobs.py` as the only internal caller, but the docstring change is the only API-level signal for external consumers.
findings.why_tests_missed_it: No test covers external callers. The updated test_fetch_explicit_ms uses timeout=1500 which is an unambiguously millisecond value and does not probe the legacy-seconds scenario.
findings.fix: Declare a deprecation or provide a migration guide for external callers; consider a keyword-enforced parameter name change (`timeout_ms`) to make the unit self-documenting and prevent silent misuse.
